signals.headline: Mavs End 14-Game Home Losing Streak with Flagg's 45-Point Explosion Over Injury-Hit Lakers | NO | LOW confidence**
signals.raw:
Mavs End 14-Game Home Losing Streak with Flagg's 45-Point Explosion Over Injury-Hit Lakers | NO | LOW confidence**  
Cooper Flagg scored 45 points, 9 assists, and 8 rebounds to lead Dallas to a 134-128 win over the Lakers on Sunday, snapping their longest home skid in 32 years; Lakers' Luka Doncic (hamstring) and Austin Reaves (oblique) are out for the regular season, but LA (50-28) remains playof

signals.headline: IMF Rate Cut Forecast Confirmed | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
IMF Rate Cut Forecast Confirmed | NO | HIGH confidence**
The IMF's April 2, 2026 Article IV Consultation with the U.S. officially concluded that there is **"little room to cut interest rates in 2026."**[1] The Fed funds rate is projected to move only from 3.6% to 3.4% — essentially one cut's worth for the entire year.[1]

signals.headline: Based on the search results provided, the most recent major announcements are from **early April 2026**, not from today
signals.raw: Based on the search results provided, the most recent major announcements are from **early April 2026**, not from today. Here are the key developments:

signals.headline: Core Inflation Stalling | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Core Inflation Stalling | NO | HIGH confidence**
Core PCE inflation remains stuck at 2.9% (as of 2025) with projections showing it will only decline to 2.6% by end of 2026[1], falling short of the 2% target until at least first-half 2027.[1] Powell explicitly warned of higher inflation in coming quarters due to energy prices from the ongoing Iran conflict.[1]
matched_markets: Will Powell say "Tariff inflation" during April press conference?

signals.headline: Tariff Rate at 80-Year High | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Tariff Rate at 80-Year High | YES | HIGH confidence**
As of April 2, 2026, the U.S. average effective tariff rate stands at **11.0%, the highest since 1943** (excluding 2025).[4] This is creating upward price pressure and constraining economic activity.[1]

signals.headline: Raptors' Brandon Ingram Delivers Historic Playoff Dud in Game 2 Loss to Cavs | NO | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Raptors' Brandon Ingram Delivers Historic Playoff Dud in Game 2 Loss to Cavs | NO | MED confidence**  
In first-round Game 2 on Monday, Ingram posted 7 points (3-15 FG), 5 turnovers, and -15 plus/minus in 36 minutes, marking one of the worst playoff games ever (first since 2016 with <8 pts on ≤20% FG, 12+ misses, 5+ TOs); Cavs took a series lead after Game 1 win.[3][4]

signals.headline: GDP Growth Modest | MIXED | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
GDP Growth Modest | MIXED | HIGH confidence**
The IMF projects U.S. GDP growth of 2.4% in 2026, a modest pickup from 2.0% in 2025,[1] but growth is expected to decay toward 1.8% thereafter.[1] Tariffs are dragging on growth, with the effective tariff rate at 7-8.5%.[1]
